entry.title: Re: Learning Hub Subscription Purchasing Error (VAL_0101)
entry.content:
Hi,

I was in similar situation couple of years back (and last month) when i tried to buy learning hub as an individual. Since you have less than a week to keep current with your certifications, Fastest way to get subscription in by contacting one of your local SAP Learning partners , they can buy subscription with your SAP ID.  Partners usually need 2-3 working days (in India) to get your LHub activated.

If you have already raised a ticket with SAP i guess they can help you by directing you to a learning partner and expedite the purchase and activation.

Someone from SAP please take a note, I think SAP should seriously consider allowing individual users (who are not having a Job like me with no learning subscription from customer/partner) to buy LHub directly. Without this we individuals will end up lose time looking for partners and risk losing our certification validity.

Regards,

Jagan.

entry.title: Re: Automating the Extraction of SAP CI DS security audit...
entry.content:
There is a relevant update on this subject; the CI-DS server version 2603 allows a CSV download of the Security Log content via Administration tab > Security Log tab. This new feature appears on the Kyma platform only, CI-DS Orgs that have not migrated from the original Neo platform will not receive this feature until they migrate to Kyma.

This CSV download still requires an authorized user to access the UI and click a button, but it streamlines access to this security audit log significantly, it does not introduce any API to obtain the audit information.

entry.title: Create Reservations for Self Service Procurement for Cost Center and Project
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community, 

We are using app "My Purchase Requisitons" (F1639A) and want to create reservations for requisitions with materials on stock with account assignment cost center and project. 

We are able to create either reserviations for projects (movment 221) or cost center (movement 201) by doing all settings in F2278 Settings for Creation of Reservation. 

However somehow it seems that it is not possible to do both settings in the app at the same time so that I can create reservations for projects (with 221) and for cost center (with 201) movment type. 

Please let us know how those settings can be done to achieve both behaviours. 

Thank you.
Best regards. 
Andreas Schwaiger

entry.title: What’s new in Lifecycle Management for SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602 ?
entry.content:
SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602

 


This release brings powerful Lifecycle Management (LCM) enhancements that streamline how you manage, access, monitor, and secure your SAP Business One environment,
delivering greater efficiency for admins and a smoother experience for end users. 

Key highlights in FP 2602 include:
  • License consumption tracking in License Control Center
  • Certificate expiration visibility directly in System Landscape Directory
  • DATEV export capability in Web Client, powered by Job Service with the new Job Monitor app
  • Support User Session reminders in Web Client
  • Component version visibility in System Landscape Directory
  • New standalone components: Webhook Messenger and Microsoft 365 Integration Service

and more...

Watch the video below to see these updates in action:
▶️ Latest Lifecycle Management (LCM) enhancements in SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602

 

Explore the full details in the What’s New guides:

  • What's New (SAP Business One)
  • What's New (SAP Business One, Version for HANA) 

💡Coming soon:  On my next blog I will walk you through the latest Identity and Access Management (IAM) innovations with Feature Pack 2602,  stay tuned!

entry.title: Re: Unable to start the server
entry.content:
Hi @deva346 ,

Hope you resolved the issue or else you can follow below troubleshoot steps .

The cause would be Incomplete Installation ,previous process may not have shut down cleanly or macOS-Specific Permissions

#Verify Server Process Stat
ps aux | grep hybris

#Check if Tomcat ports are in use (default: 9001, 900
lsof -i :9001
lsof -i :9002

#Check for lock file
ls -la hybris/data/

#Clean Server Stat
Force kill any remaining hybris processes

Navigate to platform directory
cd hybris/bin/platform

##Clean temporary files and lock
rm -rf hybris/data/hsqldb/*.lck
rm -rf hybris/data/hsqldb/*.log
rm -rf tomcat/work/*
rm -rf tomcat/temp/*

# Verify script and restore default wrapper
cd hybris/bin/platform/tomcat/bin

# Check if wrapper.sh exists and is executable
ls -la wrapper.sh

# If wrapper.sh is missing or corrupted, restore from commerce suite default
# Compare with a fresh installation or backup

# Ensure execute permissions
chmod +x wrapper.sh
chmod +x ../conf/wrapper.conf
chmod +x ../../hybrisserver.sh

# Verify directory structure
ls -la hybris/bin/platform/tomcat/conf/

Restart Server Properly
Navigate to platform directory
cd hybris/bin/platform

Start server
/hybrisserver.sh start


Regards,

entry.title: S/4HANA vs C4C as Leading System for Business Partner in Financial Services Scenario
entry.content:
Hola expertos,

Me gustaría recibir orientación basada en las mejores prácticas de SAP con respecto a la arquitectura correcta para la creación y replicación de socios comerciales (clientes) entre SAP S/4HANA y SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C).

Contexto:

Actualmente estamos diseñando una solución para una institución financiera (entorno cooperativo/bancario) con las siguientes características:

  • SAP S/4HANA como sistema ERP central (sin MDG implementado)
  • SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C) para procesos de ventas y servicio
  • Integración a través de SAP Integration Suite (CPI)
  • Sólidos requisitos regulatorios y de cumplimiento, que incluyen:
    KYC (Conozca a su cliente),
    AML (Antilavado de dinero),
    PEP (Personas Políticamente Expuestas),
    FATCA/CRS
    , regulaciones APNFD,
    listas negras y listas de vigilancia internacionales,
    relaciones familiares y partes relacionadas,
    beneficiarios con porcentaje de propiedad
    , datos financieros y personales ampliados con múltiples registros.
  • El modelo de socio comercial incluye:
    Relaciones complejas (familia, grupos vinculados, beneficiarios)
    Atributos y validaciones regulatorias
    Extensiones personalizadas para necesidades operativas y de cumplimiento
  • SAP S/4HANA es el sistema donde:
    Procesos financieros
    Datos legales y contractuales
    Lógica empresarial central (banca central)
    Controles de cumplimiento y normativas

son ejecutados y gobernados.

Pregunta:

Desde la perspectiva de la arquitectura y las mejores prácticas de SAP:

¿Cuál es el sistema principal (sistema de registro) recomendado para Business Partner?

  • S/4HANA → C4C (replicación)
  • C4C → S/4HANA
  • Enfoque bidireccional

Preocupaciones específicas:

  1. Gobernanza de datos:
    Sin los ODM, ¿debería S/4HANA seguir siendo el registro maestro?
  2. Cumplimiento normativo:
    En un escenario de servicios financieros, ¿se recomienda centralizar Business Partner en S/4HANA debido a la auditabilidad y el control?
  3. Patrones de integración
    Los escenarios de integración estándar de SAP parecen posicionar a S/4HANA como el sistema backend líder.
    ¿Sigue siendo este el enfoque recomendado para C4C?
  4. Riesgos de usar C4C como sistema de origen
    Inconsistencias de datos
    Conflictos de propiedad
    Limitaciones en el manejo de relaciones complejas y atributos regulatorios
  5. Replicación bidireccional:
    ¿En qué escenarios la recomienda realmente SAP?
    ¿Cuándo debe evitarse?

Nota adicional:

Entendemos que, técnicamente, ambos sistemas pueden crear socios comerciales. Sin embargo, queremos validar el modelo de gobernanza adecuado y la estrategia del sistema principal, teniendo en cuenta especialmente el cumplimiento normativo, la propiedad de los datos y la escalabilidad a largo plazo.

Gracias de antemano por sus aportaciones.

If you’d prefer to control it via script, you can use the corresponding API function below:

GanttChart_1.setTotalHorizon();

entry.title: Re: CA_OC_OUTPUT_REQUEST_SRV S4 Public Cloud
entry.content:
Good day @Tisha_Haria 

Thank you for your question.

You should not treat CA_OC_OUTPUT_REQUEST_SRV as a released external integration API in Public Cloud. It appears to be an internal OData service that is not released in Public Cloud with a standard predefined communication scenario. In Public Cloud external integrations are supposed to use released APIs and their associated communication scenarios/arrangements.

You cannot grant the missing authorization manually in Public Cloud as you would do in on-premise or private cloud. Your 403 error indicates the service start authorization check is failing for that OData service. In Public Cloud, you do not fix this by directly assigning backend authorization objects to make an internal service consumable from outside.

See the following references for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6d57d4ab71e5408581e2018320a0ce49/8ac548ba2cb04e2bb30d77b59fecb242.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/7139c1648de84c0bbd7637589ed7c92a.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Juntada SAP en Asuncion - Territorio Chopp Las Mercedes
entry.content:
¡Hey SAPeros de Asunción!

¡Ya vamos por la tercera juntada de la comunidad SAP Paraguay! Lo que empezó como una aventura en Territorio Chopp ya se convirtió en una tradición.
Y esta edición tiene sabor especial, porque convocamos a todos: a los veteranos ABAPeros que llevan años escribiendo reports y BAPIs, a los SAPeros de todos los módulos y áreas funcionales... ¡y especialmente a los nuevos RAPeros! que están construyendo con ABAP RAP y CAP, modelando CDS Views, exponiendo OData Services y lanzando apps en BTP como si nada.

El plan es simple: nada de corbatas ni presentaciones formales. Solo buena onda, buena cerveza artesanal y conversaciones reales sobre el ecosistema SAP — desde el clásico SE38 hasta el último release de BTP. Tal vez conozcas a tu próximo colega de proyecto, a un futuro socio, o simplemente pases una noche genial con gente que entiende lo que significa lidiar con un dump a las 11 de la noche. 

La comunidad SAP Paraguay sigue creciendo, juntada a juntada. La comunidad somos todos — clásicos y modernos, funcionales y técnicos, on-premise y cloud.

Detalles importantes:
Fecha: 25 de Marzo de 2026
Hora: 19:30 hs
Lugar: Territorio Chopp Las Mercedes
Ubicación: https://maps.app.goo.gl/Qa8tkumLLcsBJQ2y9

Confirmá tu asistencia en la sección de RSVP.
Nota: Cada uno es responsable de su consumición.

¡No te lo pierdas!
¡Nos vemos pronto! 
Zami

entry.title: Re: 1H 2026 Product Release- Browse courses or search lea...
entry.content:
Hi @RadhikaD : Below are the responses to your questions.

  1. For now, the search is supported for "Required Learning" , "Important Learning", "Requests for Me", "Invest in Myself" & "Bookmarks" sections where you have the "View All" option. It allows you to filter from the list of results you have when you click on "View All" button.
  2. In this scenario, the search result also depends on the keyword. For example,
    • If the user is searching with English keyword, but since the title is in German, the result wouldn't show up.
    • But, if the user is searching with German keyword even though his preferred language is English, the result would still show up.

entry.title: Re: U.S SECURE 2.0 Act - Section 603
entry.content:
Hello @amrice 

If I understand correctly, you have a scenario where a single plan includes both pre-tax and post-tax contributions, and you're concerned that the catch-up amount should be considered as post-tax but currently is not. Is that the case? The categorization of the catch-up amount as post-tax or pre-tax depends on the Processing Class 65 settings for the wage type in use and whether the plan allows for rollover. Essentially, whether the catch-up amount is treated as post-tax or pre-tax is determined by your system's configuration.

Regarding the 457 catch-up, it is a different type of plan and does not interfere with the 401k catch-up contribution. The 457 catch-up limit is determined by the constant 457CL, whereas the 401k plan's catch-up limit is defined by the constant 401CL. Both constants can be found in view V_T511P.

Best regards,
Gisele

entry.title: How to configure AES signature - Onboarding integrated with SAP
entry.content:
Dear community, 

We have problems with the DS EU Advance signature of DocuSign when integrated to SFSF.

Is there any other configuration that we need to set in order for this document to be signed when this type of signature is set by default??

When this document is sent to the onbaordee, is not getting to their email. But if the Simple Signature is set by default, the document is getting to the onboardee correctly. 

Waiting for your response, 

Many thanks

entry.title: BP Extension - Search By Email in ECC 6.0
entry.content:
The Client was having a legacy system where they can search the for particular Vendor if its created or not based on email ID. So moving forward they came up with requirement to centralize the Vendor Management System by migrating all things into ECC.

The first requirement came to make BP screen as it was in legacy system where user can search for Partner numbers created in system based on Email Id provided. I went through many SAP notes and found multiple approaches.

Approach

  • SPRO config – Search Criteria
SAP Customizing Implementation Guide → Logistics - General → Cross-Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Business Partner Search → Define Search Criteria for Business Partner



OR

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide → Logistics - General → Cross-Application Components → SAP Business Partner → Business Partner Search → Basic Setting → Search → Define Search Views

Note: These will only work if the BP Search Framework is fully active

  • BUS config

  • LOCA_CUST

The above 2 options didn’t worked due to lower SAP version. So the only option was to explore LOCA_CUST.

Here is the details of all technical objects created and mapping to LOCA_CUST.

  1. Create a Database View (SE11)
    Since email addresses live in table ADR6, we need a view that joins the BP to its email.
     Tables & Joins:
     BUT000 (Partner) joined to BUT020 (BP/Address Link) on PARTNER = PARTNER.
     BUT020 joined to ADR6 (Email) on ADDRNUMBER = ADDRNUMBER.

     

  2. Create an Elementary Search Help
    Once the view is active, create the search help that the BP transaction will actually display.
    SE11 -> Search Help
     Selection Method: Enter the view you just created (ZBP_EMAIL_SEARCH).
     Search Help Exit: Use F4UT_OPTIMIZE_COLWIDTH (optional, for better UI).
     Parameters:
     SMTP_ADDR: Mark as IMP (Import) and EXP (Export). LPos = 1, SPos = 1.


  3. Append to the Collective Search Help
    Now you must tell the BP transaction to include your new "Email" tab in the locator.
    Open Collective Search Help BUPA_ALL
    Go to the Included Search Helps tab.
    Click the Append Search Help button (F5).
    Create a new Append . In the "Included Search Helps" of your append, add your elementary search help

    Click Parameter Assignment to map the PARTNER field from your search help to the collective search help.



  4.  Verification
    After activating everything:
    Run transaction BP.
    In the Locator (left-hand side), click the "Find By" dropdown.
    You should now see your new entry ( with the Z name).

    NOTE: However if you click on the new field it will throw error “Program name and Screen are not configured”     

  5.  Pass the value of email field in Standard SAP flow of BP transaction
    Create Append structure in BUS_JOEL_SEARCH. SE11 ->  BUS_JOEL_SEARCH - >Append Structure(F5)


  6. Here comes the Important Step for mapping to SAP hook using ABAP code. This FM will capture the data of new field and integrate it into the standard BP process for this we need to use the OOPS concept and include some standard processing code from LBUPA_DIALOG_SEARCHF02 and LBUS_LOCATORO01.
  7. Create a Function Group. SE37-> Goto-> Function Group -> Create Group .


  8. Click on Master Program of function group and define a local class in TOP include along with the Global variable for fetching the value of email in GV_EMAIL.
FUNCTION-POOL ZBP_FMGRP_BUPA.               "MESSAGE-ID ..

* INCLUDE LZBP_FMGRP_BUPAD...                " Local class definition
TABLES bus_joel_search.

DATA: gs_dynpro_id TYPE bus_screen-area,
      gv_email type adr6-smtp_addr. " capture screen value

CLASS lcl_searcher DEFINITION FINAL.

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    CLASS-METHODS:
    select_by_email
    IMPORTING iv_search  TYPE REF TO cl_bus_locator_search
              iv_email TYPE bus_joel_search-smtp_addr
              EXCEPTIONS search_values_missing.

ENDCLASS.
  • Create a Copy FM from Standard BUPA_DIALOG_SEARCH into a  Z Function module. Follow the below code to fetch the partner from the view created in STEP1 based on input from email of BP screen.
FUNCTION zbp_bupa_dialog_emailrch.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     REFERENCE(IV_SEARCH) TYPE REF TO  CL_BUS_LOCATOR_SEARCH
*"  EXCEPTIONS
*"      SEARCH_VALUES_MISSING
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

* Local data.
  DATA: ls_search_fields TYPE bus_joel_search. 
* Convert generic container into specific fields.
  ls_search_fields = iv_search->gv_search_fields.           

* Choose the select.
  CASE iv_search->gv_search_id.

*   The empty search?
    WHEN space.
      CALL METHOD lcl_searcher=>select_by_nothing
                  EXPORTING iv_search = iv_search.

    WHEN 'ZBP_EMAIL_SRCHELP'.    "name of elementary search help
      CALL METHOD lcl_searcher=>select_by_email
        EXPORTING
          iv_search             = iv_search
          iv_email              = ls_search_fields-smtp_addr
        EXCEPTIONS
          search_values_missing = 1.

    WHEN OTHERS.
      CLEAR sy-subrc.
  ENDCASE.
*
  CASE sy-subrc.
    WHEN 1.
      RAISE search_values_missing.
  ENDCASE.

ENDFUNCTION.

​
  • Implement the method by creating a new include in Master Program.

    with the below code the Partner GUID is passed back again to BP framework to display the result of partner number based on the user input of email id. 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&  Include           ZBP_FMGRP_BUPA01
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
CLASS lcl_searcher IMPLEMENTATION.
*---------------------------------------------------------------------
  METHOD select_by_email.
*   Local data.

    DATA: lv_partner_type       TYPE bus_partner-type,
          lr_email TYPE RANGE OF adr6-smtp_addr,
          lt_partner_guids  TYPE bus_partner-guid_table.


    IF gv_email NE space.
      SELECT partner_guid INTO TABLE lt_partner_guids
               FROM zbp_email_search
               UP TO iv_search->gv_maximum_rows ROWS
               WHERE smtp_addr EQ gv_email.
*      BREAK-POINT.

      IF sy-subrc NE 0.
        TRANSLATE gv_email TO LOWER CASE.
        SELECT partner_guid INTO TABLE lt_partner_guids
             FROM zbp_email_search
             UP TO iv_search->gv_maximum_rows ROWS
             WHERE smtp_addr EQ gv_email.
      ENDIF.

    ENDIF.
*   Pass result table
    CALL METHOD cl_bupa_dialog_searcher=>add_partner_guids_to_result
      EXPORTING
        it_partner_guids         = lt_partner_guids
        iv_filter_by_search_type = space
        iv_search                = iv_search.
  ENDMETHOD.                    "select_by_email

ENDCLASS.                    "lcl_searcher IMPLEMENTATION
  • Create a new screen by Right click on Function group -> Create->Screen

    Note: GV_EMAIL is the same which is declared in step 8.Don’t forget to make the text box as scrollable otherwise you may loose data .

  • Double click on PBO and PAI and create new include in Master Program of function group. And do the following code. This code helps to pass the necessary information of standard processing copied from LBUPA_DIALOG_SEARCHF02 and LBUS_LOCATORO01.
----------------------------------------------------------------------*
***INCLUDE LZBP_FMGRP_BUPAO01.
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Module  DYNPRO_PBO  OUTPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE dynpro_pbo OUTPUT.
  gs_dynpro_id-program_name = sy-repid.
  gs_dynpro_id-dynpro_number = sy-dynnr.

  CALL METHOD cl_bus_abstract_screen=>dynpro_pbo
              EXPORTING iv_program_name = gs_dynpro_id-program_name
                        iv_dynpro_number = gs_dynpro_id-dynpro_number.

ENDMODULE.                 " DYNPRO_PBO  OUTPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&      Module  DYNPRO_PAI  INPUT
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       text
*----------------------------------------------------------------------*
MODULE dynpro_pai INPUT.
  gs_dynpro_id-program_name = sy-repid.
  gs_dynpro_id-dynpro_number = sy-dynnr.

  CALL METHOD cl_bus_abstract_screen=>dynpro_pai
              EXPORTING iv_program_name = gs_dynpro_id-program_name
                        iv_dynpro_number = gs_dynpro_id-dynpro_number.
ENDMODULE.                 " DYNPRO_PAI  INPUT
​
  • Create a new include in Master Program of function group and enter the below code. These subroutines are called from the BUS_JEOL_LOCATOR framework hence it is required in our FM.
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       FORM bus_locator_get_search_fields                            *
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM bus_locator_get_search_fields                          "#EC CALLED
     USING    iv_search_application TYPE bus_locator-search_application
     CHANGING ev_search_fields      TYPE bus_locator-search_fields.

 CASE iv_search_application.
    WHEN cl_bupa_dialog_searcher=>gc_search_application_bupa.
      ev_search_fields = bus_joel_search."#EC ENHOK
  ENDCASE.

ENDFORM.

*---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*       FORM bus_locator_set_search_fields                            *
*---------------------------------------------------------------------*

FORM bus_locator_set_search_fields                          "#EC CALLED
     USING    iv_search_application TYPE bus_locator-search_application
              iv_search_fields      TYPE bus_locator-search_fields.

  CASE iv_search_application.
    WHEN cl_bupa_dialog_searcher=>gc_search_application_bupa.
      bus_joel_search = iv_search_fields. "#EC ENHOK
  ENDCASE.

ENDFORM.
  • Functional Configuration in LOCA_CUST

    Create new entry under Search by ID

    Search for the Search Help created in Step gy doing an F4 on Locator Search ID.

    Screen text would be displayed on the drop of Find by screen of BP .Now do F4 for FM and Program name and enter screen number created in step 11 the details. That’s the end for the required config for mapping the new field to BP screen.



  • Let’s test
    Enter Tcode BP. Click on BY dropdown to see the new field available under name Email.

    Enter any relevant email Id to find the respective BP . You can find the BP from the view created in Step 1 as well.

    Result

entry.title: Architecting a Clean-Core, Agentic Exception-Handling Pattern for SAP Order Fulfillment
entry.content:
Business scenario at a glance

Real-world problem: A customer order is accepted in SAP S/4HANA, but a downstream shortage, carrier constraint, or credit/logistics exception threatens the promised delivery date. The enterprise wants faster, AI-assisted resolution without pushing new custom logic into the ERP core. This is where Clean Core 2.0 and Agentic AI become one architecture pattern rather than two separate buzzwords [1]–[7].

Architectural point: S/4HANA remains the system of record for sales orders, ATP, pricing, delivery, and financial posting. The reasoning layer sits side-by-side on SAP BTP, consumes released APIs and business events, grounds recommendations on trusted enterprise context, and writes back only through governed interfaces [1], [3]–[8].

 

Figure 1. Clean-core responsibility split for order-fulfillment exceptions

The figure above makes the design boundary explicit: the ERP core executes the business transaction, while the side-by-side layer performs contextual reasoning and orchestrates bounded actions. That partition aligns with SAP clean-core extensibility guidance and reduces the chance that AI becomes another form of hidden customization [1], [2].

Runtime architecture for the real scenario

At runtime, the architecture works in six steps. First, S/4HANA creates or updates the sales order and produces the standard business outcome. Second, an approved event or API call exposes the change to SAP Integration Suite and, where needed, advanced event mesh. Third, a Joule-based or SAP BTP agent service classifies the situation as a fulfillment exception. Fourth, the agent gathers business context from governed sources such as policy documents, logistics rules, customer commitments, and curated data products. Fifth, it proposes a bounded action, such as split shipment, alternate source proposal, or carrier-switch recommendation. Sixth, the approved action is written back through released APIs or workflow paths into the transactional landscape [3]–[9].

Figure 2. SAP agentic exception-handling runtime architecture

This layered model matters because it defines where trust controls sit. SAP’s Joule architecture, grounding patterns in SAP AI Core, and responsible AI guidance all point toward a model in which enterprise context, policy, and approval must be explicit architectural controls rather than afterthoughts [3]–[7].

Concrete order-to-cash example

Example: A distributor promises a same-week delivery for a high-priority customer order. Minutes later, inventory in the planned warehouse is consumed by another allocation, and the preferred carrier misses its cut-off window. In older landscapes, planners would manually piece together ATP, stock-in-transit, customer priority, freight rules, and contractual penalties across multiple screens. In the target design, the agent assembles that same context automatically and recommends one of three pre-approved actions: (1) partial shipment now plus backorder, (2) reallocation from a secondary warehouse, or (3) carrier substitution with cost impact shown to the approver [1], [3]–[9].

Figure 3. Real-world order-fulfillment exception flow

Final Thoughts

The business case shared above answers a concrete architecture broader question: what should stay in the ERP core, what should move to a side-by-side intelligence layer, and how do we preserve compliance, upgradeability, and operational control? The scenario is representative of real order-to-cash programs in manufacturing, distribution, and consumer products, where exception handling speed directly affects OTIF, logistics cost, and customer experience.

References

[1] SAP, 'Clean core extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud,' 2024. URL: https://www.sap.com/docs/download/2024/09/20aece06-d87e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.pdf

[2] SAP, 'RISE with SAP | ERP Clean Core Strategy,' 2025. URL: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/rise/methodology/clean-core.html

[3] SAP Learning, 'Understanding Joule Architecture and Extensibility,' 2025. URL: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/provisioning-and-implementing-joule/understanding-joule-architecture-and-extensibility

[4] SAP Developers, 'Orchestration with Grounding Capabilities in SAP AI Core,' 2025. URL: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-orchestration-grounding..html

[5] SAP, 'Responsible AI,' 2025. URL: https://www.sap.com/products/artificial-intelligence/ai-ethics.html

[6] SAP, 'SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh,' 2025. URL: https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/integration-suite/advanced-event-mesh.html

[7] SAP Help Portal, 'SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh Releases,' 2026. URL: https://help.pubsub.em.services.cloud.sap/Release-Notes/PubSub-Cloud-Release-Notes.htm

[8] SAP TechEd workshop, 'Real-time data for AI agents: Grounding with event-driven architecture,' 2025. URL: https://github.com/SAP-samples/teched2025-IN162

[9] SAP News Center, 'Shape the Future of Intelligent Applications with SAP Business Data Cloud,' May 21, 2025. URL: https://news.sap.com/2025/05/future-intelligent-applications-sap-business-data-cloud/

entry.title: Please Suggest an API or BOI to create and update Purchase Order Confirmation

Also function to mass upload.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Please Suggest an API or BOI to create and update Pur...
entry.content:
Hi,

Supplier confirmations are separate business documents besides purchase orders. The API can be used to create and change these supplier confirmations. When a supplier confirmation is created, the purchase order is updated as it is the case, when adding confirmations to a purchase order with the app “Change Purchase Order - Advanced”.

Currently, the CDS I_POSupplierConfirmationAPI01 is available to get details about confirmations of a purchase order.

Refer to 3401990 - Supplier Confirmation API For Purchase Order - SAP for Me

Also note with this service mass processing is not supported.

One supplier confirmation can be created, changed, or deleted per POST, PATCH, and DELETE operation.

Refer to Supplier Confirmation - OData V4 | SAP Help Portal

Thanks and regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Clarification required on Hyperscale DTU usage limits and potential additional charges
entry.content:
We need clarification regarding DTU usage behavior after the migration to Hyperscale. Since DTU consumption is now configured to auto‑scale based on usage, we would like to understand the following:

  • Will there be any additional charges if the DTU usage crosses a certain threshold?
  • Or is the usage unlimited, with no extra charges beyond the existing plan?

We also require assistance with Dynatrace monitoring:

  • The full usage details/dashboard are currently not visible in Dynatrace.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Clarification required on Hyperscale DTU usage limits...
entry.content:
Thank you for reaching out to SAP Commerce Technical Support.

Let me try to answer your questions:

  • Will there be any additional charges if the DTU usage crosses a certain threshold? --- The Azure Hyperscale in commerce cloud us using provisioned mode, which means it always has available set amount of Vcores as per configured and are not scaled up or scaled down automatically. Which means the cost of them always remains the same and is not affect on less or high use.
  • Or is the usage unlimited, with no extra charges beyond the existing plan? - Usage is limited to set limit, unless scaled up. The big improvement if required to scale up the limit of vcores is 128 which is relativetly 4 times higher than limit of 4000 DTU before and the max storage size can be used upto 100TB from 4TB before.
  • The full usage details/dashboard are currently not visible in Dynatrace. - you can find the details about usage and limit in SAP - Performance Monitoring v2 Dashboard besides DB an& Solr Service.
  • Also you can use KBA 3620510 How to check the VCore metrics after transitioning from DTU to VCore, to find the metrics in Dynatrace

entry.title: Note 933420 is incorrect about ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE limitations
entry.content:
The note 933420 - ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE - SAP for Me is misleading:

  • The output structure is limited to 9999 rows and columns.

I trust the indications given in this question/answer ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE confusion - SAP Community:

  • nearly 70.000 rows
  • 256 columns...?

In fact, to understand why the note says "9999", or what it means, one have to look at ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE, it returns an internal table (parameter INTERN) with one line per value, which has this structure (ALSMEX_TABLINE):

  • ROW (4 digits)
  • COL (4 digits)
  • VALUE (50 characters)

So, ROW and COL may have a value ranging from 0 to 9999, starting from 1 for the first row.

What the note doesn't say, is that it doesn't stop returning the Excel values at the row 9999, if more lines exist in the Excel file they are returned, the ROW number is 0 for the line # 10.000, 1 for the line # 10.001 and so on.

NB: the note says that you may copy the function module to a custom one and change the type of the internal table ALSMEX_TABLINE to a custom type with the three components ROW, COL and VALUE but define them with an increased length as per your requirements.

Do you have any precise information about the real limitations other than what said here? (and possibly why it's limited to around 70000 and 256)

Thanks.

Sandra

NB: I know this function module is not supported by SAP and should not be used (it's also what the note says).

entry.title: Complicated Freight charges
entry.content:
Hello,

Our client will be using basic TM (s/4 hana embedded). It does not use LE-TRA for shipments.

There are 15 transporters and there are fifteen contracts with these transporters with different terms and conditions  for freight calculation.

for one of the transporter below are the conditions,

a) For location from  X to Y location, the freight cost will be INR 13 per kg. However if a particular delivery has less than 10 kg, a minimum weight of 20 kg will be charged Plus INR 500 as fixed charge due to weight less than 20 KG.  Also there is fixed charge of Rs 100 per way bill.  One truck will have one way bill. Many deliveries for ship to party Y can be in one way bill.

b) If the location is from X to Z location, the freight cost will be INR 12  per kg. However if a particular delivery has less than 10 kg, a minimum weight of 20 kg will be charged Plus INR 500 as fixed charge due to weight less than 20 KG.  Als o for destination location Z, a fixed surcharge of INR 1500 will be charged. Also there is fixed charge of Rs 100 per way bill.  One truck will have one way bill. Many deliveries for ship to part Z can be in one way bill.

How to design charge management to meet the above requirement ?

rgds

sachin

entry.title: How an I traverse thru tables and fill a result table?
entry.content:
BusinessOne objects in SAP rest in a bunch of tables, that have a numeric ID but a text name - for whatever reason.
The first function just gets me the correct table:

CREATE FUNCTION "q_GetDocLinesTable"
/**
 * Get B1 objects table of a documents lines
 * V1.0 Coded 2026-02-05 by .rhavin;)
 */
(
	/* B1 Document Type */
	IN ObjType int
)
	/* B1 Document Meta Table */
	RETURNS TableName NVARCHAR(10)

	LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT 

AS

BEGIN

	IF     (:ObjType=13) THEN TableName := 'INV1'; /* A/R Invoice */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=14) THEN TableName := 'RIN1'; /* A/R Credit Memo */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=15) THEN TableName := 'DLN1'; /* Delivery */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=16) THEN TableName := 'RDN1'; /* Returns */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=17) THEN TableName := 'RDR1'; /* Sales Order */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=18) THEN TableName := 'PCH1'; /* A/P Invoice */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=19) THEN TableName := 'RPC1'; /* A/P Credit Memo */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=20) THEN TableName := 'PDN1'; /* Goods Receipt PO */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=21) THEN TableName := 'RPD1'; /* Goods Return */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=22) THEN TableName := 'POR1'; /* Purchase Order */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=23) THEN TableName := 'QUT1'; /* Sales Quotation */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=24) THEN TableName := 'RCT1'; /* Incoming Payment */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=67) THEN TableName := 'WTR1'; /* Incoming Payment */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=540000006) THEN TableName := 'PQT1'; /* WTF? */
	ELSE TableName := NULL;
	END IF;
End;

I also created a type that I need to fill:

CREATE TYPE "q_tt_EntryMap" AS TABLE (
	"Entry" INTEGER,
	"Type" INTEGER
);

Now, what I want to is:

  • Step 1: Start with any given combination of Entry/Type
  • Step 2: fill a resulting table of type q_tt_EntryMap with the combination (if found)
  • Step 3: if any "BaseEntry": move to that combination and goto Step 2.

The following function gets me the first entry, but I'm unsure how to append to my result-table and how to move on to the next entry:

CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE "q_GetEntryTable"
(
EntryID int,
EntryType int
)

LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT 
SQL SECURITY INVOKER

AS
	LineTable     NVARCHAR(10);
	tSQL          NVARCHAR(4000);
	Result        "q_tt_EntryMap";

BEGIN

	LineTable := "q_GetDocLinesTable"(EntryType);
--	WHILE (:LineTable IS NOT NULL)
--	BEGIN
		tSQL := '
		SELECT DISTINCT "BaseEntry" AS "Entry", "BaseType" AS "Type"
		FROM ' || :LineTable || '
		WHERE "DocEntry" = ' || :EntryID || ' AND "BaseType" <> -1
		';
		EXECUTE IMMEDIATE tSQL into Result; /* ## How do I append? ## */
--		LineTable := "q_GetDocLinesTable"(/* ## Somehow put Type-Field of last row of :result into it ## */);


--	END;
		
	SELECT * FROM :Result;

END;

 

So if [1234,15] has a BaseEntry of [2345,13] and that has a BaseEntry of [3456,17] (I just made up those numbers), the resulting table returned should look like this:

(Entry)(Type)
123415
234513
345617

Of course, if there is a better way to do this, please feel free to tell me.

entry.title: How to create an enhancement spot of type source code plugin
entry.content:
I am surprised that GenAI is completely unable to answer correctly to my question, always answering SE18 or SE20 or whatever, which is wrong, probably it has not been asked or answered enough in the Web. So let me fix that with the question + the correct answer.

My question: "how to create an enhancement spot of type source code plugin".

I'm not talking about one of type BAdI.

I'm talking about one which can be used to create an enhancement point or section (enhancement-point or enhancement-section xxxx spots yyyy, where yyyy is the enhancement spot).

Sandra

entry.title: Re: How to create an enhancement spot of type source code plugin
entry.content:
To create an enhancement spot of type source code plugin, do as below. Note that this operation MAY also create an enhancement point or section at the same time (OR MAY NOT see below (*)).

  1. Use the SAP GUI IDE (SE38, SE80 or whatever) - You may also use ADT but after displaying any source code, use Open with > SAP GUI
  2. Display any source code
  3. Switch to change mode
  4. Right-click on any line of the source code
  5. Select Enhancement Operations > Create Option:
  6. Type the names of the enhancement point or section, and of the enhancement spot with package, possibly the transport request if the package is transportable, and press Enter:
  7. You can see the statement inserted in the source code:
  8. Activate the source code
    • (*) NOTE: if you don't activate and leave without saving, you'll see that only the enhancement spot has been created anyway, and not the enhancement point (it's what I experienced in S/4HANA 2023 FPS01)

NB: via ADT, I guess it's impossible natively, only one of type BAdI can be created, as you can see:

entry.title: Re: Syncronizing the batch Numbers in both EWM & S4
entry.content:
Hi,

you would typically divide the full range up in (at least) 2 intervals, one internal and one external.

The 2 intervals then have to be mutually exclusive on both sides.

E.g.

  1. ERP, interval 01, internal, 00000001 to 49999999
  2. ERP, interval 02, external, 50000000 to 99999999
  3. EWM, interval 01, external, 00000001 to 49999999
  4. EWM, interval 02, internal, 50000000 to 99999999

So, if you create a batch on the ERP side it will be, for example 00000555 (from the internal range). When you transfer the batch record to EWM it will accept that number because for EWM it is within the external range.

Hope that helps

Andreas

entry.title: Re: Unable to Sync Users from SuccessFactors to SAC due t...
entry.content:
Hi!

To synchronize Analytics users using Role-Based Permissions Synchronization, it is essential to execute and schedule the refresh synthetic group job in SAP SuccessFactors.

Within SuccessFactors, navigate to the Schedule Job Manager, Job Scheduler, and Create Job Request. Select the Job Type as Refresh Synthetic Group Data and specify “Run for All” as the run criteria

This job should be executed once, and subsequent runs can be scheduled based on the desired frequency. Each time permissions are granted to Story Reports for a user, they will become accessible after the job execution and IPS synchronization.

You can follow step 10 of the following guide: 
https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-platform/story-reports/syncing-users-from-sap-successfactors-hcm-suite-to-sap-analytics-cloud

entry.title: Complicated carrier charges
entry.content:
Hello,

Our client will be using basic TM (s/4 hana embedded). It does not use LE-TRA for shipments.

There are 15 transporters and there are fifteen contracts with these transporters with different terms and conditions for freight calculation.

for one of the transporter below are the conditions.

a) For location from X to Y location, the freight cost will be INR 13 per kg. However if a particular delivery has less than 10 kg, a minimum weight of 20 kg @ INR 13 per kg will be charged.Plus INR 500 as fixed charge due to weight less than 20 KG. However Lets say there are two deliveries for ship to Y. the total weight for two deliveries is 18 kg, so carrier will charge for 2 kg more @ INR 13 i.e 26 Rs more. But then fixed charge will be charged as INR 500 (fixed charge for less than 20kg ) minus INR 26 already charged i.e INR 474. Also there is fixed charge of Rs 100 per way bill. One truck will have one way bill. Many deliveries for ship to party Y can be in one way bill.

b) If the location is from X to Z location, the freight cost will be INR 12 per kg. However if a particular delivery has less than 10 kg, a minimum weight of 20 kg will be charged Plus INR 500 as fixed charge due to weight less than 20 KG. Also for destination location Z, a fixed surcharge of INR 1500 will be charged. Also there is fixed charge of Rs 100 per way bill. One truck will have one way bill. Many deliveries for ship to party Z can be in one way bill.

How to design/configure charge management to meet the above requirement ?

rgds

sachin

entry.title: Re: Create Reservations for Self Service Procurement for...
entry.content:
Dear @andreasschwaiger_consilio,

In Public Cloud the standard setup does not support determining two different reservation movement types within the same Settings for Creation of Reservation (F2278) rule based on the purchase requisition account assignment category. SAP documents automatic reservation creation in F2278 and in My Purchase Requisitions as being controlled by a purchasing group + material group combination. In other words the published standard determination key is purchasing group/material group, not cost center versus project. The standard reservation scenarios themselves are different: SAP documents movement type 201 for cost-center-related reservation posting and SAP documents movement type 221 for reservation to project stock. However, SAP Public Cloud documentation does not describe a standard F2278 rule that automatically switches between 201 and 221 according to the requisition account assignment

If the business requirement is to support both behaviors in standard the recommended approach is to split the process using the configuration dimensions that SAP actually supports, for example by using different purchasing groups and/or material groups for the two scenarios. A practical example would be one purchasing group/material group combination for cost center requisitions that creates reservations with 201 and a different purchasing group/material group combination for project requisitions that creates reservations with 221

Summary:

If the requirement is to keep the same purchasing group and same material group and have the system derive 201 for cost center and 221 for project automatically from the PR account assignment this should be treated as a standard limitation in Public Cloud, rather than as a missing configuration step

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/713eae2544204816b71a88caa89bd256.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/d99983576ea1a96be10000000a4450e5.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/32da8359c8ee4e8b8e8c5e15cacba5aa/31be7e548af58e4ce10000000a4450e5.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Data Replication Proxy - Time Off
entry.content:
We have Data Replication Proxy set up to replicate absences. We recently added a new absence but forgot to add it to the Data Replication Configuration list of time types. We have now added it, but need to generate the proxies for the absences that were created before the Data Replication Configuration was updated.

In the Data Replication Configuration, there is a field for Execute Data Replication Proxy job. However, the documentation says that if it is set to Yes, it will create proxies for all relevant absences, even if they have already been created. This does not sound like what we want.

The alternatives suggested in documentation are to use Trigger Data Replication Proxy Creation Job, but I only see an option to do that by user, not by the time type. The other suggested alternative is to make a change to each Time Off/Absence record, which could be rather time consuming. 

Another alternative that I am wondering about is creating a new Data Replication Configuration record with only the new absence type, set the Execute Proxy Creation to Yes, and then delete this new record and let the existing Data Replication Config record pick up any newly created absences going forward.

Does anyone else have a suggestion on how this can be handled? 

Thank you,

Cindy

entry.title: How to allocate fixed % (e.g. 4%) via SKF in cumulative CO distribution w/o changing cycle settings
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I would appreciate some advice on a CO distribution scenario that I have been testing extensively, but I have still not found a clean and reliable solution for.

Business requirement

I need to set up a distribution cycle in SAP CO with the following target logic:

  • 4% of the sender cost center costs should be allocated to the receiver cost center
  • 96% should remain on the sender cost center

The key requirement is that I do not want to control this via Sender Values – Share in %, because the business wants to be able to change the percentage monthly without having to modify the cycle each time.

Instead, the idea is to use Statistical Key Figures (SKF) so that the percentage split can be maintained dynamically per period.

Important context

The cycle is set up as a cumulative cycle.
This is one of the main reasons why I am trying to avoid changing fixed percentages in the cycle itself after execution.

What I am trying to achieve

My target setup would ideally work like this:

  • Sender cost center posts 100% of the relevant costs
  • Receiver gets only the portion defined for the month (for example 4%)
  • The remaining amount (for example 96%) stays on the sender automatically
  • Future changes, such as 5% / 95% or 6% / 94%, should be possible via SKF maintenance only, without changing the cycle

My challenge

In my tests, I have not found a clean way to achieve this behavior through SKF-based distribution.

What I tested in principle was:

  • Sender Values = 100%
  • Receiver structure including:
    • receiver cost center
    • sender cost center again, so that the remainder is “sent back”
  • Tracing factor based on SKF
  • SKF values such as:
    • receiver = 4
    • sender = 96

Technically, this seems to work only in a very indirect way, because effectively 100% is distributed and 96% is allocated back to the sender.
From a design perspective, this feels quite error-prone and difficult to govern, especially in a cumulative cycle environment.

Why I am concerned

My concerns with this setup are:

  1. The logic becomes difficult to explain and validate, because the sender is also part of the receiver structure.
  2. It seems prone to maintenance errors. If the sender is accidentally omitted from the receiver structure, the result could be completely wrong.
  3. In cumulative cycles, I am also concerned about the impact of changing SKF ratios during the year and whether this could create unexpected “catch-up” effects or inconsistencies.

What I want to understand

I would like to ask the community:

  1. Is there a standard or best-practice way in SAP CO Distribution to allocate only a small percentage (e.g. 4%) to a receiver while automatically leaving the remainder on the sender, without changing the Sender Share % in the cycle?
  2. Is using SKF with sender also included as a receiver the only technically feasible approach if monthly flexibility is required?
  3. How do you handle this in cumulative cycles, especially if the percentage changes during the year?
  4. Is there a cleaner alternative that allows:
    • no cycle maintenance for percentage changes
    • period-dependent allocation ratios
    • no risky “send 100% out and return 96% back” logic?

What I already understand

From my current understanding, there seems to be a trade-off:

  • Fixed percentage in Sender Values
    • simpler and more transparent
    • but not flexible, because cycle maintenance is required for each change
  • SKF-based setup
    • more flexible, because percentages can be maintained per period
    • but technically more complex and potentially error-prone, especially in cumulative cycles

If anyone has implemented a similar requirement successfully, I would really appreciate your guidance on the recommended design.

Thanks a lot in advance.

entry.title: SIT RECIFE - 10 anos - O que você espera para este evento?
entry.content:
Nos reunimos mais uma vez com o objetivo de alinhar os próximos passos para um dos momentos mais marcantes da nossa comunidade: a edição de 10 anos do SAP Inside Track Recife.

Mais do que um evento, o SIT Recife representa uma trajetória construída desde 2016, consolidando-se como um ponto de encontro essencial para troca de conhecimento, networking e evolução do ecossistema SAP na região .

Durante nossa reunião, reforçamos o compromisso em elevar ainda mais o nível desta edição especial, trazendo conteúdos relevantes, experiências práticas e fortalecendo o espírito colaborativo que sempre caracterizou o SAP Inside Track. A expectativa é entregar um evento que conecte profissionais, inspire novas ideias e celebre a força da comunidade que vem crescendo ano após ano.

Seguimos avançando com entusiasmo e propósito, construindo juntos mais um capítulo dessa história.

entry.title: Option to Delete Scheduled Jobs from the My Jobs Page in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Option to Delete Scheduled Jobs from the My Jobs Page in SAP SuccessFactors 

Introduction

 As part of H2 2025 SAP SuccessFactors has introduced a useful enhancement that allows users to delete scheduled jobs directly from the My Jobs page. This update improves job management and helps keep the job list clean and organized. This feature is based on a Customer Influence Idea and has been officially published on the SAP site.

 What’s New

 You can now delete scheduled jobs for all report types—except Canvas reports—directly from the My Jobs page.

 This enhancement allows users to efficiently manage and organize their scheduled jobs without waiting for automatic cleanup.

 What’s Changed

 Current Version:

 You can delete scheduled jobs for all report types, except Canvas reports.

 Jobs can be deleted from: View Schedules - My Jobs page When you click Delete, a confirmation dialog appears.

 Delete option is disabled if the job status is: Started, In Progress, and Waiting.

 

 Previous Version:

 Jobs could not be deleted from the My Jobs page.

 Old jobs stayed visible for 14 days and were removed automatically.

Pre-requisites

RBP Permissions: Administrator Permissions > Manage Dashboards/Reports > View All Schedules.

Why this matter

This update gives users more control over their scheduled jobs. Instead of waiting for old jobs to expire, users can now manually clean up their job list, making it easier to track active and important jobs.

Conclusion

  • Enhanced User Experience: The new Delete option on the My Jobs page delivers a smoother and more intuitive user experience.
  • Improved Efficiency: Users can quickly remove outdated or unnecessary job entries, reduce manual effort and save valuable time.
  • Streamlined Job Management: The feature simplifies day‑to‑day operations, making job list maintenance more efficient.
  • Customer‑Driven Innovation: This enhancement reflects SAP’s continued focus on listening to customer feedback and delivering meaningful, user‑centric improvements.

 Enhancement request: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/224710
